observations.recommendation:
We commended Management for complying with its duties as withholding and
remitting agent of taxes in accordance with RMC No. 23-2012 dated February 14,
2012. However, we recommended that the Accounting personnel in charge strictly
monitor and determine the exact amount to be remitted each month to avoid
under/over remittances.

We commended the Management for (i) accurately, completely, and properly
recording and documenting all related transactions; and (ii) consistently preparing
and submitting BRS. Nevertheless, we recommended that the MA take necessary
action to adjust the stale checks amounting to ₱142,700.00 to correct balances of the
CIB-LCCA and the corresponding payable account.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA to (i) ensure that the
disbursements are duly supported with complete documentation; (ii) submit to the
AT the deficient documentation; (iii) strictly monitor the CAs granted by requiring
the SDOs to liquidate their CAs including the unspent balances in their possession
within the reglementary period; and (iv) record the advances to SDOs for various
purposes under Advances to SDO account.

We recommended that Management undertake the following courses of action
a. instruct the MA to (i) prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to
correct the recording of advance payments of RPT and SET:
Particulars Dr. Cr.
Discount on Advance Payments ₱1,021,156.72
Deferred RPT ₱510,578.36
Deferred SET 499,445.48
Discount on SET 11,132.88
(ii) make use of the Certified List of Taxpayers and Taxes Due to be submitted
by the MT as the basis for setting up the RPT and SET Receivables and
Deferred Credits at the beginning of the year; and (iii) trace and record the
necessary adjustments to reconcile the discrepancies between the amount of
the recorded RPT and SET Receivables and the related Deferred Credits
accounts; and
b. instruct the MT to (i) expedite the updating of List of Delinquent Real
Property Taxpayers; (ii) continuously monitor receivables by sending demand
letters on the delinquent accounts and intensify efforts on collection to increase
RPT and SEF collection efficiency; (iii) consider the other tax remedies
provided in R.A. No. 7160 such as continuous posting the notice of the
delinquency at the main entrance of the Municipal Hall and in publicly
accessible and conspicuous places in each barangay of the LGU concerned;
and (iv) furnish the MA of a duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers
and the amounts due and collectible for the year to serve as the basis of
recording.

We recommended that the LCE:
a. instruct the MA to (i) maintain SLC for each type of inventory items; (ii)
record all procurement of inventory supplies in the books of accounts using
the Perpetual Inventory System; (iii) draw the necessary journal entries to
recognize the amount of Drugs and Medicines and Medical, Dental and
Laboratory Supplies Inventory as at year-end amounting to ₱647,128.75 and
₱69,050.00, respectively; and (iv) be more vigilant to ensure that issued
supplies and materials were recorded to appropriate expense accounts;
b. instruct the Supply Officer and other concerned offices to (a) maintain SC for
each item of inventory; and (b) prepare SSMI to be submitted every week or
at least once a month to the MA together with RIS or equivalent document so
that issuances will be computed and recorded by the latter; and
c. create an Inventory Committee that would facilitate the conduct of physical
count of inventory items by type semestrally and will prepare the
corresponding RPCI.

We recommended that the LCE:
a. advise the MA to draw the necessary journal entries to adjust the improperly
recorded depreciation of various PPE;
b. create an Inventory Committee who will spearhead the conduct of the annual
physical count of PPE and require the same to prepare and submit the
RPCPPE to the AT not later than January 31 of each year. Likewise, strategize
the conduct of one-time cleansing of PPE account balances pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2020-006; and
c. direct the Property Officer to (i) prioritize the registration/renewal of all
motor vehicles owned by the Municipality with the LTO to prevent possible
loss and misuse of the assets and to avoid incurring penalties, fines or sanctions
from the LTO; and (ii) facilitate the insurance of all motor vehicles owned by
the Municipality to ensure the LGU is indemnified or compensated for any
damage or loss due to possible casualties that may happen on these insurable
assets.

We recommended that Management direct the:
a. MA to (i) draw a JEV to record the initial set-up of provision for earned leave
credits in the books of accounts of the ensuing year as recommended below:
Account Name Dr. Cr.
Prior Period Adjustment ₱23,927,957.30
Leave Benefits Payable ₱23,927,957.30
(ii) be guided by the suggested journal entries in accounting for transactions
related to leave benefits as illustrated in Annex C.1.3 of COA Circular No.
2024-009;
b. HRMO Head to (i) initiate and prioritize the reconciliation and correction of
balances in the employee leave cards; (ii) revisit its process for updating and
maintaining leave cards. Specifically, the revised process should include
regularly updating leave cards and other internal control measures to provide
timely and accurate updates; (iii) strictly comply with the provisions of CSC
Memorandum Circular (MC) No. 41, s. 1998 and No. 16, s. 2002 on the monetization of the leave credits of
employees. Likewise, discontinue the practice of allowing the monetization
of SL credits unless warranted by the circumstances provided under CSC MC No. 16, s. 2002; and (iv)
submit to the AT the SDs of employees who opted to monetize 50% or more
of their accumulated leave credits during the CY 2024;
c. enjoin the MA and the HRMO to institute a periodic audit of all employees’
accumulated leaves before approval and payment of their respective
monetized leave credits; and
d. set aside additional reserve funds for the accumulated compensated leave
credits as of year-end; henceforth, ensure the adequacy of the funds provided
for the leave credits earned thereafter by including the said benefits in the
annual budget.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA and other LGU officials
concerned to:
a. undertake a one-time cleansing of the Due to NGAs account by following the
guidelines and procedures for the write-off of dormant accounts, specifically
those from unknown SA;
b. regularly update the SL to ensure accurate tracking of transactions;
c. prepare the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers to adjust the erroneous entries
and correct the improperly recorded transactions; and
d. execute and submit the MOA for all fund transfers, which shall contain the
guidelines and procedures in implementing projects by the
implementing/recipient LGUs, including their responsibilities.

We recommended that the (a) OIC-Municipal Planning and Development
Coordinator (MPDC) and the Municipal Engineer (ME) properly plan and closely
monitor the implementation of the development projects to ensure the timely and
orderly execution thereof; (b) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and ME submit a
written explanation on the noted deficiencies on the project Construction of Line
Ditches/ Canal and Cross Drainage; (c) BAC require the winning contractor to
submit their DOLE-approved CSHP within the prescribed period; and (d) MA to
perform a thorough review of all disbursement claims and ensure only those with
complete documentation are processed and presented for payment.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
a. LDRRM Officer to closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned
PPAs are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the
70% MF for pre-disaster preparedness programs so that the intended purpose
is attained/achieved; and (ii) implement stricter monitoring and regular
inspection of stockpiled goods to avoid spoilage and wastage;
b. MA to (i) perform a thorough review of all disbursement claims and ensure
only those with complete documentation are processed and presented for
payment and (ii) make necessary journal entries to correct the improper
recording of stockpiled goods; and
c. Municipal Engineer submit a written explanation on the noted deficiencies on
the project Construction of Flood Control Project at Barangay Binabalian,
Candelaria, Zambales.

We commended the Management for its compliance with budget
appropriation for PS within the limitation set by R.A. No. 7160 and for maintaining
complete documentary requirements for claims of salaries and wages of Casual, COS
and JO personnel. However, to further improve personnel management and
strengthen internal controls, we recommended that the Management instruct the
Municipal HRMA to (a) prepare a Statement of Duties and Responsibilities for
Casuals and COS personnel to evaluate their accomplishment vis-à-vis assigned
task/duties; (b) submit the required locator slips/travel orders and leave applications,
if any, to verify their absence during headcount and ensure proper accountability;
and (c) provide the AT with copies of the contract, DTRs and accomplishment reports
of the COS personnel.

We recommended that the LSB (a) ensure that the dates of transmittal of copy
of SEF Budget to the concerned departments are clearly indicated in the receiving
copy on file; and (b) submit the quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget
Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B) on SEF utilization, as prepared by the MA, to
the DepEd CO, through appropriate channels, copy furnished the local sanggunian
concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MA and the MT including all AOs
to (a) strictly adhere to the timeframes on the submission of accounts and financial
reports; (b) immediately transmit the unsubmitted DVs and SDs; and (c) impose
appropriate sanctions against those officials/employees concerned who have
continuously disregarded the set deadline for the submission of the accounts/reports.

We recommended that the LCE to:
a. instruct the LGU GFPS to monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs to
ensure that the same are implemented as planned to attain GAD’s goals and
objectives;
b. issue an appropriate policy directive, copy furnished to the DILG Office that
conducted the review of the LGU GPB, to ensure the dissemination and
implementation of the GPB; and shall mandate the LGU GFPS to: a) provide
technical assistance in the implementation of the GPB; b) monitor its
implementation; c) ensure the preparation and submission of GAD AR; and
d) consolidate reports on the implementation of the GPB; and
c. instruct the GFP to submit the documentary requirements supporting the
GAD Accomplishment Report.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MA and persons
responsible/liable to immediately enforce the settlement of audit disallowances
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

observations.managementAction: The preparation of a stratified schedule of PIL was not included in the 2025 Revised Revenue Code of the Municipality. As of this date, no book of accounts has been thoroughly examined. No personnel have been hired exclusively for the purpose of conducting these examinations. Taxpayers are required to submit sworn statements of their gross sales/receipts for the preceding CY or quarter to verify the actual sales. On December 04, 2024, SB enacted the 2025 Revised Revenue Code of the Municipality. However, the Ordinance relative to the collection of Situs Tax on outside contractors was not revised and was not included in the 2025 Revised Revenue Code of the Municipality.

observations.managementAction: The MA commented that all individuals held liable had settled their disallowances through payroll and Terminal Leave Benefits deductions over various periods starting in CY 2015, except for the former Sangguniang Kabataan Chairwoman. The unsettled amount, totaling ₱22,000.00, remained outstanding as her term had already ended before the notice was served. The MAO further explained that settlement of this disallowance with the Commission could not proceed, as several DVs and SDs could no longer be retrieved. The MA inquired whether, in lieu of the payroll records and DVs a certification could be issued to confirm that the concerned individuals had already settled their obligations. However, the AT recommended exerting further effort to retrieve the missing DVs and SDs to substantiate the settlement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
a. refrain from granting additional CAs if the previous CAs were not fully
settled or accounted for. Consider possible withholding of salaries of those
officials and employees who persistently refuse to cooperate and did not
liquidate their CAs within the timeframe as required by the present
regulation;
b. instruct the MA to (i) strictly monitor the CAs granted by requiring the
personnel concerned to liquidate their CAs including the unspent balances
in their possession within the reglementary period; (ii) record the advances
for official travel of officers and employees under Advances to Officers and
Employees account; and (iii) ensure that the liquidation of CAs was duly
supported by a LR and submit the same to the AT for verification; and
c. instruct the concerned officials and employees to liquidate their CAs within
the reglementary period.

We commended the management for its substantial compliance with the
provisions of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 relative to the
granting of CA for CF. However, we recommended that the MA record the CA for
CF under the Advances to SDO account instead of Due from Officers and Employees
account to avoid misstatement of Receivable accounts.

We recommended that the LCE:
a. advise the MA to (a) draw the necessary journal entries to (i) recognize the
fully delivered equipment amounting to ₱2,489,500.00 under DRRE
account and its corresponding depreciation amounting to ₱118,251.25; (ii)
reclassify the installation of CCTV from ICT Equipment to Military, Police
and Security; and (iii) derecognize/drop in the book the value of the
donated/disposed of motor vehicles; and (b) prioritize the updating of the
property and accounting records including PPE SL and lapsing schedule,
and immediately present the same to the Office of the Auditor for
verification and review;
b. advise the SO to intensify efforts for the timely conduct of annual physical
count of PPE and submit the required Reports of PPE to COA within the
deadline set; and
c. regularly renew the registration of all serviceable motor vehicles of the
Municipality with LTO and contracts of insurance with GSIS to avoid
additional expenses in terms of penalties and payment of damages to third
parties in case of accidents.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA to:
a. prioritize the updating of the property and accounting records
particularly the SL of CIP – Infrastructure Assets and immediately
present the same to the Office of the Auditor for verification and review; and
b. prepare a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to reclassify the completed project
from the CIP to the appropriate PPE account and recognize the
depreciation from the date of completion.

We recommended that the LCE advise the MA to (i) maintain SL for the Due
to LGUs and Due to NGAs accounts and immediately present the same to the Office
of the Auditor for verification and review; (ii) make representations with the
identified SAs to facilitate the proper liquidation and/or return of fund transfers; (iii)
conduct cleansing of the accounts by following the procedures for the write-off of
dormant accounts, specifically those from unknown source agencies; (iv) draw
necessary journal entry to return the balances from completed projects to the GF for
the unspent LGUs counterpart funds; and (v) draw journal entry to correct the
erroneous recording of fund received from DOH.

We recommended that the Management (i) instruct the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) to ensure that the timeframe for each procurement stage/activity
has been strictly followed; and (ii) require the MA to process claims on the advance
payment/mobilization fee only upon submission to and acceptance by the procuring
entity of an irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial
bank, a bank guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand as prescribed by
Section 4.2 of Annex E of the 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that the LCE:
a. direct the LDRRMO to (i) closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM
planned PPAs are implemented and give due regard on the maximum
utilization of the 70% MF for pre-disaster preparedness programs so that
the intended purpose is attained/achieved; and (ii) include in the
MDRRMFIP the list of PPAs to be undertaken out of the unexpended
LDRRMF of previous years under STF; and
b. in coordination with the LDRRMO and other key officials strictly
implement the provisions of Section 28, Article II and Section 7, Article III
of the 1987 Philippine Constitution, COA Circular 2013-004, and Annex H
of the 2016 RIRR of R.A. No. 9184 when resorting to Negotiated
Procurement under Emergency Cases.

We recommended that management:
a. strictly implement the Municipal Ordinance No. 05 and impose fines and
penalties for violators;
b. conduct an extensive information education campaign that would educate
and encourage the active participation of the constituents on proper waste
segregation;
c. ensure that waste collectors are equipped with personal protective
equipment to free them from hazards of handling solid wastes;
d. exert effort to terminate the MOA with the Envirotech Waste Recycling,
Inc.; and
e. expedite the purchase of needed parts of the equipment to repair it and to
be able to push the pile of garbage into the designated disposal area.

We recommended the LCE to:
a) instruct the MT to (i) prepare a stratified schedule of PIL every tax period
to approximate the gross sales or receipt of each business classification and
submit a representation to the SB to set up or institutionalize the use of the
same; (ii) regularly examine the books of accounts of business
establishments to determine the truthfulness of the declared gross sales or
receipts used in the computation of business taxes paid pursuant to Section
171 of R.A. No. 7160; (iii) assign personnel at the MTO who are well-
equipped with the necessary knowledge and training in the examination of
books of accounts to enable the said Office to carry on with their mandate
efficiently; (iv) tag all the afore-cited suppliers and contractors with
discrepancies in the sales or receipts and be subject to examination of
books of accounts to ensure the correctness of taxes, fees, and charges to
be collected; and (v) strictly impose the 2% monthly interest on delinquent
business taxes;
b) advise the Designated BPLO to require the submission of complete
documentation of the financial records from taxpayers for the correct
assessment of business taxes; and
c) consider revising the Ordinance relative to the collection of Situs Tax on
outside contractors in accordance with the existing laws.

We recommended that the LCE (a) ensure that all funds received from source
agencies are supported with duly signed MOA/TA to clearly define the specific
guidelines and procedures in the implementation of individual projects, including the
responsibilities of the Municipality, thereby ensuring timely accomplishment thereof
for the benefit of the intended beneficiaries; (b) advise the MA to coordinate with the
BAC for the completion and submission of the documentary deficiencies noted and
ensure that DVs are supported with complete documentation before payments are
made; (c) instruct the LGSF Focal Person and the Municipal Engineer to prepare the
plans and programs taking into account the provisions of the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184 on
the conduct of pre-engineering study before undergoing the detailed engineering to
ensure the viability of the proposed projects, thereby preventing constant revision of
the approved plans and programs; and (d) provide written explanation on the noted
deficiency relative to the approval of time extension not in accordance with the
provisions of RIRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that Management:
a. establish the GAD Database and ensure complete and timely
implementation of GAD PPAs so that desired results and benefits for the
intended recipients/beneficiaries are fully achieved;
b. strive for the full implementation of GAD-related PPAs by utilizing
judiciously the budget set aside for said purposes; and
c. ensure that GAD AR is supported with the requirements mentioned under
Section 5.C.8.5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that Management direct the MA and the MT including all
AOs to (a) strictly adhere to the timeframes on the submission of accounts and
financial reports; and (b) immediately submit the aforementioned unsubmitted DVs
and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• General Services Officer to complete and update the property cards for
each property, plant and equipment and complete the necessary
information and data to be reflected in the RPCPPE which will be used
in the reconciliation of PPEs;
• OIC-Municipal Accountant to update the PPELC for each category of
plant, property and equipment;
• Municipal General Services Officer and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to
pursue and complete the reconciliation of the accounting with property
records in order to establish the correct PPE balances, to investigate and
adjust any discrepancy that will be noted; and
• Disposal Committee to undertake the immediate disposal of the
unserviceable properties to avoid further deterioration and possible loss
and to generate additional income from its disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their
records and make the appropriate adjustments for the fair presentation
of the accounts in the financial statements; and
• Municipal Accountant to maintain and update Subsidiary Ledgers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal
Accountant to reconcile the balance of Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account
against the LDRRMF Utilization Report and effect any adjustment, if
warranted.

1.8	We recommended that Management instruct the:
a.	Municipal Treasurer to prepare a schedule of all unreleased checks every end of the year for submission to the MAO; and
b.	Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments for the restoration to the respective Cash in Bank account based on the schedule of unreleased checks submitted by the Municipal Treasurer at the end of the year to arrive at the correct balance of the Cash and other related accounts.

2.22	We recommended that Management instruct the:
Municipal Accountant to (i) maintain SLC for each commodity/stock; (ii) record all procurement of inventory supplies in the books of accounts using the Perpetual Inventory System; (iii) draw the necessary journal entries to recognize the amount of Drugs and Medicines and Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies Inventory as at year-end amounting to ₱409,974.00 and ₱6,000.00, respectively; and (iv) be more vigilant to ensure that issued supplies and materials were recorded to appropriate expense accounts; and
Supply Officer to (i) maintain stock cards for each item of inventory; (ii) regularly reconcile the stock cards with the SLCs of the Accounting Office; (iii) report the issuances of supplies thru the SSMI supported with RIS and/or proof of distribution of goods and supplies and furnish a copy thereof to the Municipal Accountant which shall be the basis in recording to appropriate Expenses accounts; and (iv) conduct a physical count of stocks of all supplies and materials and submit relative reports to the Office of the Auditor and other offices concerned on a semi-annual basis.

4.12	We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) revert to the General Fund all dormant/long outstanding Accounts Payable which have been outstanding for two years or more and against which no actual claims have been filed or which are not covered by perfected contracts on record; (b) conduct a periodic review to ensure that the Accounts Payable consists only of valid and legitimate claims against the Municipality; and (c) draw a JEV to correct erroneous classification of payables.

5.11	We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Health Officer and staff with the coordination of the Human Resource Management Officer and Municipal Accountant to submit the list of eligible frontliners duly approved by the Local Health Board, DTRs, duties, and responsibilities with accomplishment reports and Appointments/plantilla of regular, casuals and JOs personnel for us to evaluate the validity and propriety of the grant and disbursement of the AHDP and SRA.

6.21	We recommended that Management coordinate with the MSWDO to set well-defined policies and clear-cut guidelines on screening, verification, assessment and documentary requirements to promote more equitable cremation assistance.

7.13	We recommended that Management a) advise the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the BAC for the completion and submission of the noted documentary deficiencies and ensure that only disbursement vouchers with complete documentation are processed and presented for payment; and (b) oblige the contractor to conduct repair on the damaged road projects to restore quality assurance of the project.

8.32	We recommended that Management:
direct the LDRRM Officer to (i) strive for the timely and full implementation of the planned disaster-related PPAs to achieve the goals and objectives of the fund and the desired benefits; (ii) include in the MDRRMFIP the list of programs, projects and activities to be undertaken out of the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years under Special Trust Fund; and (iii) furnish the Office of the Auditor with Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF within the reglementary period; and
instruct the Municipal Accountant to perform a thorough review of all disbursement claims and ensure only those with complete documentation are processed and presented for payment.

10.26	We recommended that Management:
strictly comply with the Municipal Ordinance No. 05 by creating and recruiting SWM enforcers; encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable waste; undertake proper information dissemination like the conduct of seminars and production of posters/tarpaulins on the harmful effects to public health of the smoke from burned solid wastes as well as the prohibited acts enumerated in the Municipal Ordinance; and
exert effort to renew/amend the MOA with ENVIROTECH Waste Recycling, Inc. and sort out the issues regarding its terms and conditions specifically the repair of the damaged equipment for its effective use; and
require the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to comply with their commitment(s)/promise(s) to DENR-EMB III to avoid the cease-and-desist order against the operation/s of the LGU’s actual and/or potential sources of pollution.  The payment of a fine/penalty of ₱105,738.44 should be shouldered by the person/s or official/s concerned determined to be liable for the non-implementation of certain provisions of laws, rules and regulations.

11.21	We recommended that Management:
establish the GAD Database and ensure complete and timely implementation of GAD PPAs so that, desired results and benefits for the intended recipients/beneficiaries are fully achieved;
formulate the LGU GAD Code in support to the LGU’s efforts in promoting, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights, women’s economic empowerment and gender-responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality and women’s empowerment.
ensure that GAD AR is supported with the requirements mentioned under Section 5.C.8.5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

12.11	We recommended that Management (i) assign personnel under the Mayor’s Office who will be in charge of the issuance and monitoring of gas slips; (ii) install an adequate internal control system starting from the process of filing a request to the issuance and monitoring of gasoline/diesoline withdrawn by the different end-users; (iii) ensure that the Driver’s Trip Tickets are properly accomplished and duly signed by the required signatories; and (iv) prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption.
12.12	We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant refrains from processing payment for claims without complete documentation/signatures to ensure the legality and validity of the disbursements made.

13.7	We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and BAC Chairman to (a) immediately transmit to the Audit Team the unsubmitted accounts and financial reports; and (b) strictly comply with the timeframes on the rendition of accounts and preparation/submission of financial reports including copies of perfected contracts/purchase orders pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and other pertinent issuances.

14.15	However, we recommend to (a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of withholding 5% VAT on payments made for deceased Senior Citizens; and (b) instruct the institutional partner to effect a mandatory 20% discount and VAT exemption on transactions involving Senior Citizens to avoid sanctions provided under Section 7 of RA No. 9994.

15.8	We commended the efforts of the Accounting and Treasury Departments and recommended to continue to be compliant with regulations on withholding and remittance of monthly PhilHealth contributions within the prescribed period.

17.8	We appreciate the effort of the Accounting and Treasurer’s Offices for substantial remittance of premiums and loan amortizations to Pag-IBIG during the year. However, we recommended (a) strict coordination between two offices so as not to overlook remittances; and (b) management include the Pag-IBIG MID number as one of the requirements to be submitted by a newly hired employee before the release of his/her salary to avoid the accumulation of unremitted premiums as well as the incurrence of penalty/ies in the future.

18.5	We recommended that Management instruct the persons liable to settle the remaining audit disallowances pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 pending the result of the appeal filed at COA RO III.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the required financial reports and documents for CY 2020 within the prescribed period. Henceforth, ensure that the succeeding reports and documents are submitted within the prescribed period pursuant to existing rules and regulations.

(a) continue to adopt the good practice of promptly remitting premiums, loan amortizations, and other contributions to the Home Development Mutual Fund; and 

(b) instruct the personnel concerned to exert more effort in the analysis of the outstanding balance of ₱1,529.68 and cause the remittance thereof once the correct amount and details are duly established.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality complied with the deadline for remittance of premiums, loan amortizations and other contributions.
observations.reason:
Detailed verification of the outstanding balance is ongoing. 

Reiterated the observation in Part II of the CY 2020 AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal General Services Officer to:
  • conduct a complete physical inventory of all the Municipality’s assets and to prepare and submit the required report thereon; and
  • prepare and maintain property cards for each category of PPE; and
• Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each category of assets.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Office (GSO) performed physical inspection to take account of each office’s assets and has prepared a corresponding report. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant started the preparation of PPELC in compliance with the prior year’s audit recommendations.
observations.reason: In the RPCPPE prepared by the GSO, details such as unit values and acquisition costs of some PPE items are not presented. Likewise, there was inappropriate classification of property, plant and equipment by type. The property cards were not completed and updated as of CY 2020. The Municipal Accountant has started the preparation of PPELCs and requested for more time to reconcile the variances noted due to the bulk of assets.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal General Services Officer to coordinate with the ICO-Municipal Engineer to prepare the road map of the Municipality to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and reporting of the Municipality’s road network system for inventory via Geographic Information System (GIS);
• ICO-Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and
• Municipal Accountant to record the amount of road networks required in the transitory provision of COA Circular No. 2015-008 for the fair presentation of the Road Network accounts in compliance with Paragraph 27 of IPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The General Services Office (GSO) was in continuous coordination with the Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) regarding the preparation of road maps of the Municipality; the MEO together with the Municipal Planning and Development Office and the GSO have provided road pins and developed a map showing the road network system of the Municipality for inventory via GIS and transmitted the report to the Office of the Municipal Accountant and the GSO; the MEO has accomplished identifying the Municipality’s road networks and its components together with their respective geo-tag, road IDs and plotted to the Municipality’s road network plan in GIS format and transmitted the copy of the document to the GSO and the Municipal Accountant and a report on the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects shall be prepared and submitted to the GSO and Office of the Municipal Accountant; and the Office of the Municipal Accountant is currently reconciling all the accounts with the submitted records and shall complete the records once the full report from the MEO is transmitted.
observations.reason: The ICO-Municipal Engineer has provided the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer with the Road Network Inventory. However, recording of amounts required in the transitory provision was not achieved until CY 2021; with new developments the audit observation was updated under Part II of the Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the lacking supporting documents to the Audit Team to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit; and
• Municipal Assessor to expedite the titling of the land in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor submitted to the Audit Team a copy of the Certificate of Title in the name of the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant processed, prepared and accomplished the necessary documents following the guidelines and procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal General Services Officer to maintain complete and updated stock cards per type of supply and to prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued together with the original copy of Requisition and Issue Slip to the Municipal Accountant for the recognition of the expenses; and
• Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for the inventory items.
We also recommended provision for storage room/stock room in the Municipal Hall for deliveries of inventories to be held in stock.
observations.managementAction: The GSO released a letter advising all offices of the new procedure in its effort to centralize the management of inventory of supplies and materials; through the help of each office, they shall keep a record of all the supplies and materials and set up stock cards to accomplish the required documents; and work with the Office of the Municipal Accountant to report the expenses relative to the supplies and materials of Municipality’s offices. The Office of the Municipal Accountant shall work with the GSO to start the preparation of ledger cards. The GSO, together with the Municipal Engineering Office, has already set up a storage room/stock room at the second floor of the municipal hall to safekeep, manage, monitor and eventually, centralize the inventory of supplies and materials of the local government unit offices.
observations.reason: Completion of stock cards is ongoing and the Supplies Ledger Cards were not fully prepared/maintained due to skeletal workforce arrangement because of the COVID-19 pandemic; some actions started only in February 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor request the Sangguniang Bayan for a Resolution authorizing him to file the request for write-off of dormant receivables; and require the Municipal Accountant to support the request for authority to write-off dormant receivables following COA Circular No. 2016-005 and set up the provision for Allowance for Impairment in order to present fairly the Loans Receivable-Others account at net realizable value consistent with COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. 277-2020 was issued authorizing the Municipal Mayor to file the request for write-off of dormant receivables of the Municipality. The Municipal Accountant processed, prepared and accomplished the necessary documents following the guidelines and procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005. The Municipal Accountant set-up the provision for Allowance for Impairment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• require the Municipal Accountant to check the validity of non-moving prior years’ balances of Inter-Agency Payables account, complete the reconciliation of the amount withheld/deducted against the amounts remitted then remit the valid payables and adjust those without supporting documents for fair presentation; and
• instruct the Municipal Treasurer for the prompt remittance of the provincial shares from property, special education, professional and transfer tax and barangay shares from RPT and CTC.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant has requested for additional time to reconcile and justify the discrepancy on the Inter-Agency Payables account due to the bulk of documents needed to be reviewed and that most of these transactions were from years ago. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office will prepare the necessary documents to ensure prompt remittance of provincial shares.
observations.reason: There were delays in remittances due to the COVID-19 pandemic and the Municipal Accountant was unable to complete reconciliation because many transactions dated from prior years and voluminous documents needed to be reviewed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• instruct the Municipal Treasurer to continuously coordinate with the Information Technology Section as well as with the service provider to solve/address the problems encountered with the system;
• designate administrative staff from the available manpower to assist the Municipal Treasurer in Real Property Tax related transactions; and
• require the Municipal Treasurer to continually enforce the collection of the delinquent real property taxes by conducting more aggressive tax campaign and offering amnesty and incentives for full payment and by availing of remedies provided in Sections 256, 258 and 260 of RA No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer’s Office will continuously coordinate with the Information Technology Section to look into options in solving the problem and will continue to mobilize the Barangay Treasurers to disseminate information, send out Notice of Delinquencies and assist in the campaign on taxes/fees collection. Human Resource Management will review current workload and identify administrative staff to assist the MTO. ITS will coordinate with the service provider for more efficient report generation.
observations.reason: The service provider was not able to act on their request and no additional personnel were assigned due to constraints and pandemic-related disruptions; issuance of Notices of Delinquency was halted in CY 2020 and resumed in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal GAD Focal Point System (MGFPS) to submit to the Audit Team the Accomplishment Report within five working days from the end of January of the preceding year for audit and evaluation purposes pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-001.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Point System Focal Person submitted to the Audit Team the Accomplishment Report for Gender and Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal General Services Officer to ensure that all the insurable properties of the Municipality are covered with appropriate insurance with the GSIS in compliance with COA Circular No. 92-390 and RA No. 656 by requiring the Municipal Engineer to expedite the completion of necessary documents required by the GSIS to facilitate the application for property insurance of all the properties of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: General Services Office will continue to attend to the application for the insurance coverage of other municipal properties such as dry market section of the public market and continue to collate pertinent documents relative to municipal vehicles. Municipal Engineering Office will prepare necessary documents required by the GSIS and work closely with the General Services Office.
observations.reason: Some municipal properties remained uninsured as completion of required documents was still in process; renewal and new coverage of several properties and motor vehicles were accomplished in CY 2020 but other properties like school buildings, hospitals and other structures remained uninsured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Planning and Development Coordinator to ensure that the 20% Development Fund be optimally utilized to implement prioritized programs, projects and activities (PPAs); install regular monitoring mechanism to assess progress of project implementation; ensure closer coordination among involved officials; and formulate monitoring templates.
observations.managementAction: The Planning and Development Office initiated the reactivation of the Project Monitoring Committee to ensure effective and efficient execution and monitoring of development PPAs. The office formulated templates to be used as part of regular monitoring and assessment of programmed PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk and Reduction Officer to submit to the Audit Team the Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Management Fund on or before the 15th day of the ensuing month in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-009 and Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balance of Trust Liabilities – DRRMF against the records of the LDRRMF Report once available and effect any adjustment; and disclose the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO submitted the required report to the Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant was able to reconcile the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account with the records of the DRRMF Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to install effective and sound accounting controls over the implementation of projects by administration by adopting the following courses of actions; to wit:

a.	Instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize inventories upon acquisition of construction materials based on the Inspection and Acceptance Reports;





b.	Require the Supply Officer to (i) prepare Requisition and Issue Slip every time materials are issued based on actual quantities requested; (ii) maintain stock cards for construction materials which balance shall be reconciled regularly with accounting records; and (iii) conduct periodic inventory-taking of inventories; and





c.	Direct the Municipal Engineer to prepare accomplishment reports and status of project completion as the basis for the recognition of the Construction in Progress Account and the closure thereof to appropriate PPE accounts after project completion.
observations.reason:
Municipal Accountant did not recognize inventories upon acquisition of construction materials based on the Inspection and Acceptance Reports. Still, all materials procured were debited to the Construction in Progress account, regardless of actual requisition made per project.
The Supply Officer did not prepare Requisition and Issue Slip every time materials were issued based on actual quantities requested; stock cards for construction materials were not yet maintained; and periodic physical count of inventories was not conducted.

Reiterated the observation in Part II of the CY 2019 AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to instruct the SWMB to 
(i) exert effort in the revision of the SWMP to ensure its approval immediately in the ensuing year; 




(ii) monitor/oversee its eventual implementation, particularly the provisions of the SWMP regarding the establishment of Barangay Material Recovery Facility based on the guidelines set forth in R.A. No. 9003; and 



(iii) widen information dissemination to include the guidelines on the imposition of penalties against offenders for the purpose of making the public aware of the consequences of doing such prohibited acts.
observations.managementAction: In lieu of MRF, garbage receptacles/ trash bins have been currently used by the barangays in selected collection points to serve as temporary storage of solid wastes while awaiting collection by the Municipal Government.
observations.reason: The SWMP was not yet approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission (NSWC) and still in the process of revision in coordination with the Provincial Solid Waste Management Board (PSWMB).

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations and management agreed to 

(a) require the GFPS to (i) discharge their functions faithfully pursuant to Executive Order No. 2 and the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-001 as amended; 

(ii) adopt the HGDG in the identification of real gender issues in the Municipality; and 

(iii) prepare a rational GPB which is assessed continually against actual GAD accomplishments; and 

(b) create a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team to efficiently and effectively monitor and evaluate the outcomes of the LGU’s GAD Policies and Programs. These measures will enable the Municipality to comply with the mandate of R.A. 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women; thereby, promoting a gender responsive governance.
observations.managementAction: A GPB was prepared which was continually assessed against actual GAD accomplishment as reflected in the GAD AR
observations.reason:
The GFPS failed to discharge their functions.


No proof that HGDH tool was used in the identification of real gender issues of the Municipality.



Reiterated the observation in Part II of the CY 2019 AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require/direct:
• the ICO – General Services Officer (GSO) to continue the conduct of the physical count of all properties, exert all efforts to complete the inventory of all existing PPE and prepare the RPCPPE in the required form with the necessary information and data; and
• the ICO - GSO and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records of PPE to prepare and investigate the causes of the discrepancies in the balances of the accounts to bring the two records in agreement, and effect the necessary adjustments in both records, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal General Services Officer was unable to conduct the physical count of PPE and to submit the RPCPPE for CY 2019.

With the new developments, the audit observation is updated and presented anew under audit observation no. 1, Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
• ICO - GSO to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare the road map of the Municipality to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and reporting of the Municipality’s road network system; 
• Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and
• Municipal Accountant to record the amount of road networks required in the transitory provision of COA Circular No. 2015-008 for the fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal General Services Office was unable to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and reporting of the Municipality’s road network since the data needed are still with the Office of the ICO-Municipal Engineer.

observations.recommendation:

• SB for a Resolution to authorize him to file the request for write-off of dormant receivables; and
• Municipal Accountant to support the request for authority to write-off dormant receivables following the guidelines and procedures provided under COA Circular No. 2016-005.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor informed that the request for write-off of dormant receivables has been made. Also, the Municipal Accountant has already recorded the provision for Allowance for Impairment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team the Accomplishment Report for DRRM Fund for proper evaluation in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will submit the reconciliation on the balance of Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and the LDRRMF Report to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
1.24	We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to adjust/correct bookkeeping errors as follows:
recognize as assets all PPEs acquired out of Trust Fund by debiting the Other Structures and Furniture and Fixtures accounts for ₱8,153,455.87 and crediting Government Equity for the same amount;
close the amount of CIP – Buildings and Other Structures and CIP – Infrastructure Assets to the appropriate PPE accounts such as Markets, Hospitals and Health Centers, Buildings and Other Structures, as follows:
General Fund:
c. record the amount of accumulated depreciation of Other Structures, Furniture and Fixtures, Markets, Hospitals and Health Centers, Buildings and Power Supply System, as follows:
General Fund:
d. 	revert the entry which previously recognized the partially completed Flood Control System as PPE and the corresponding Depreciation Expense and Accumulated Depreciation; to wit:
General Fund:
prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the Installation of Solar Lights and Streetlights from Other Structures to Power Supply Systems.

2.12	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) undertake the following courses of actions:
instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to (i) ensure the completeness of all information and supporting documents submitted by the bidders during the evaluation of technical documents and eligibility check; and (ii) submit all perfected contracts with complete supporting documents for auditorial review by the Audit Team within five working days from execution pursuant to Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009; and
advise the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the BAC for the completion and submission of the documentary deficiencies of the above six projects and ensure that only DVs with adequate documentation are processed and presented for payment.

3.27	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (i) instruct the BAC and the Municipal Engineer to plan effectively and schedule properly all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to ensure timely implementation and completion thereof; (ii) direct the OIC-MPDC and the Municipal Engineer to monitor regularly the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time; and (iii) require the Municipal Engineer to accomplish the necessary Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA) for every infrastructure project implemented by administration, which will be validated by the Municipality’s Inspectorate Officer and the basis for the issuance of Certificate of Completion and Acceptance by the head of agency. Afterwards, submit to the Audit Team the SWA, Inspection Report, and Certificates of Completion and Acceptance for said infrastructure projects implemented for review.

5.23	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive undertake the following courses of action:
instruct the MDRRMO to (i) monitor and evaluate the utilization of MDRRMF based on the programs, projects and activities listed in the Work and Financial Plan; (ii) ensure compliance with the prescribed minimum standards for disaster preparedness developed by the DILG and LGA; and (iii) furnish the Office of the Auditor with the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in the required format through the LDRRMC and the Local Development Council (LDC) on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and
advise the Municipal Accountant to perform a thorough review of all disbursement claims to ensure that these are properly supported with complete documentation and proper approval by authorized officials/representatives.
5.24	Management committed to comply with the audit recommendations.

6.9	We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, including all Accountable Officers to (a) strictly comply with the timeframes in the rendition of accounts and preparation/submission of financial reports; and (b) impose appropriate sanctions against those officials/employees concerned who have continuously disregarded the set deadline for the submission of the accounts/reports.

8.14	We recommended that management, thru the GAD Focal Person, undertake the following remedial measures:
gather GAD information such as gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data to serve as inputs for planning, budgeting, programming and policy formulation for the setting up and maintenance of GAD database;
issue an Executive Order for the establishment of GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team and the composition thereof which will be responsible for the evaluation of the results of the LGU GAD policies, programs, and projects; and
intensify the monitoring of GAD-related PPAs to ensure maximization and proper application of available funds for the intended purposes so that the desired outcomes are attained and benefits are enjoyed to the fullest by the intended beneficiaries.

9.11	We recommend that management undertake the following courses of action:
instruct the Municipal Treasurer, together with other offices concerned to (i) intensify the collection of real property taxes, including other revenue sources to generate more income for the Municipality; (ii) strictly monitor the posting of the notice of the delinquency in publicly accessible and conspicuous places in each barangay of the LGU concerned; and (iii) publish the notice of delinquency once a week or for two consecutive weeks in a newspaper of general circulation of the Municipality. Proper information dissemination would help to inform taxpayers concerned of their tax obligations and ultimately make them aware of their duty to pay in accordance with existing laws; and
avail of the remedies provided under Section 256 of the Local Government Code of 1991 to enhance the collection of real property tax and enforce necessary administrative action thru levy on real property or judicial action as a last resort.
9.12 	The Municipal Treasurer assured us that they will continue to send demand letters to delinquent real property owners and strictly enforce the posting of the Notices of Delinquency in respective Barangays.

10.9	We recommended that management (a) continually abide with the BIR regulation on the deadline of remittance of taxes withheld from employees as well as from suppliers/contractors; and (b) instruct the personnel in-charge to determine the exact amount to be remitted to arrive at an accurate balance of the Due to BIR at year-end; and (c) determine the causes of over or under remittance that eventually distorted the account balance and coordinate with the BIR as regards possible offsetting of the excessive remittances against the amounts due in the succeeding remittance period.

11.7	We recommended that management (a) continually exert effort to comply with the provisions of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 8291 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations on the withholding and remittance of premium contributions and loan amortizations of employees; and (b) instruct the personnel concerned to exert more effort in the analysis of the outstanding balance of ₱6,803.09 and cause the remittance thereof once the correct amount and details are duly established.

12.7	We recommended that management (a) continue to adopt the good practice of strictly complying with the provisions of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 10606 and its IRR on the withholding and remittance of PhilHealth premium contributions; and (b) instruct the personnel concerned to determine the exact amount to be remitted to arrive at an accurate balance of the Due to PhilHealth at year-end and determine the causes of over or under remittance thereof.

13.7	We recommended that management (a) continue to adopt the good practice of promptly remitting premiums, loan amortizations, and other contributions to the Home Development Mutual Fund; and (b) instruct the personnel concerned to exert more effort in the analysis of the outstanding balance of ₱1,529.68 and cause the remittance thereof once the correct amount and details are duly established.

14.5 	We recommended that management require all persons responsible to settle immediately the audit suspensions and disallowances of ₱2,803,433.93 pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 or the RRSA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to report and record the LGU’s collections accurately and timely to ensure the reliability of the financial statements.

We recommended and management agreed to install effective and sound accounting controls over the implementation of projects by administration by adopting the following courses of actions; to wit:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize inventories upon acquisition of construction materials based on the Inspection and Acceptance Reports;
Require the Supply Officer to (i) prepare Requisition and Issue Slip every time materials are issued based on actual quantities requested;(ii) maintain stock cards for construction materials which balance shall be reconciled regularly with accounting records; and (iii) conduct periodic inventory-taking of inventories; and
Direct the Municipal Engineer to prepare accomplishment reports and status of project completion as the basis for the recognition of the Construction in Progress Account and the closure thereof to appropriate PPE accounts after project completion.
We recommended and management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to take up necessary correcting entries to recognize the remaining supplies/equipment for distribution as Inventories.

We recommended and management agreed to (a) require the BAC and the BAC Secretariat to (i) faithfully adhere to their functions as enumerated in Sections 12 and 14 of the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184; (ii) submit the required documents to the Audit Team; and (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to carefully scrutinize and review all documents attached to any disbursement voucher and make sure that the same were complete prior to certifying the completeness of supporting documents; otherwise the Audit Team will be forced to issue Notice of Disallowance (ND).

We recommended that management (a) examine carefully the facts and circumstances of the delay before approving any request for extension of contract time; (b) see to it that all procurement contracts are implemented within the period agreed upon by the contracting parties in order not to deprive the end-users and intended beneficiaries of the utility and enjoyment thereof; and (c) impose the required liquidated damages for at least one tenth (1/10) of one percent of the cost of the unperformed portion of the contract for every delay of the project.

We recommended and management agreed to instruct (a) the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to (i) prepare and submit an Annual Procurement Plan for all development projects for consolidation by the BAC;(ii) plan and schedule properly activities relating to the projects to be undertaken by administration by indicating in the Program of Works (POW) the contract time/duration; (iii)ensure that the Municipality is technically capable to handle the projects “By Administration” by taking an inventory of existing skills before the actual project implementation; (iv) include all projects/programs/activities, regardless of fund source to the Quarterly Report submitted to this Office to promote transparency and accountability.; (b) instruct the BAC and BAC Secretariat to submit the required documents to the Audit Team as enumerated in Appendix O, Part IV of this report; (c) instruct the Municipal Accountant to carefully scrutinize and review all documents attached to any disbursement voucher and make sure that the same were complete prior to certifying the completeness of supporting documents; otherwise the Audit Team will be forced to issue Notice of Disallowance; and (d) conduct monitoring of implemented projects to ensure attainment of desired socio-economic and environmental outcomes through (i) re-orientation of the barangay officials on the use and sole purpose of the Eco-Trikes; and (ii) train the dump truck driver on the high tech features of the new Dump truck to maximize its utilization for garbage collection.

We recommended and management agreed to instruct the LDRRMC to (i) refrain from utilizing LDRRM equipment/supplies other than for the intended purposes; (ii) explain/justify the discrepancies noted on the procured supplies and account properly the distribution thereof; and (iii) obtain a permit from the CENRO to legalize ownership and possession/use of chain saws pursuant to the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9175. Failure to do so by the public officials concerned may warrant their removal and perpetual disqualification from holding public office, in addition to other penalties imposed by the Act.

No. 9184 in all the procurements made out of SEF; and refrain from authorizing the payment of claims of suppliers except when all pertinent supporting documents are attached to the disbursements claims.

In this manner, projects targetted for completion would be finished and reported on time for the benefit of the intended end-users/beneficiaries.

We recommended and management agreed to (a) carefully plan the projects, programs and activities that may be included in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan to see to it that only those items that are sanctioned and authorized by R.A. No. 10121 and NDRRMC –DBM –DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013 – 1 dated March 25, 2013 are included therein to prevent illegal and irregular disbursement of public funds; and (b) cause the immediate opening of an account for the LDRRMF with the Landbank of the Philippines , Iba, Zambales pursuant to SB Resolution No. 55 dated November 13, 2017; and (c) strictly abide by the prescribed procedures for projects undertaken “ By Administration”.

We recommended that management strictly comply with the requirement on the establishment of LCPC and for the allocation and utilization of 1% IRA for children’s PPAs as mandated under Section 15 of R.A. 9364 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended and management agreed to (a) promptly submit the accounts and reports for various months and adhere strictly to the timeframes on the submission of accounts and financial reports to the Audit Team pursuant to the RRSA and COA Circular No. 2009-006; and (b) see to it that all unsubmitted accounts for CY 2017 are transmitted to the Office of the Auditor; otherwise, we shall be constrained to recommend the withholding of salaries of concerned Municipal officials/personnel.

We recommended and management agreed to (a) require the GFPS to (i) discharge their functions faithfully pursuant to Executive Order No. 2 and the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-001 as amended; (ii) adopt the HGDG in the identification of real gender issues in the Municipality; and (iii) prepare a rational GPB which is continually assessed against actual GAD accomplishments; and (b) create a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team to efficiently and effectively monitor and evaluate the outcomes of the LGU’s GAD Policies and Programs. These measures will enable the Municipality to comply with the mandate of R.A. 9710 or the Magna Carta of Women; thereby promoting a gender responsive governance.

We recommended and management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) abide strictly with the timeframe on the withholding and remittance of GSIS premium contributions; and (b)refund to the employees withheld premiums not accepted by the GSIS in the amount of ₱21,760.28 to prevent the accumulation of unremitted balances under the Due to GSIS account.

We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Market Administrator to (a) strictly enforce the provisions of the Revenue Code; (b) consistently implement the issuance and approval of Application for Lease and Contract of Lease to all stallholders; (c) maintain a complete and updated registry book containing vital information about the stallholders; i.e. names and addresses, numbers, and description of stalls occupied, location, date and time of receipt of application, and contract period; (d) consider the revision of the policies concerning the rates to be imposed and define the front corner and inside corner stalls for proper collection of the fees dues; (e) impose additional rental fees for extension of stall spaces; and (f) sustain the policy on the grant of 20% discount to eligible stallholders to encourage prompt payments. Strict implementation of the provisions of the Code would benefit the Municipality in terms of increased collections of rental fees from market stalls which could be utilized to finance the improvement of market operations and other needs of the Municipality.

We recommended and management agreed to (a) facilitate the installation of the transformer in the Public Market immediately upon delivery; (b) commence the operation of the New Public Market as soon as the needed facilities are acquired and renovations were done; and (c) take appropriate actions to attract consumers to visit and purchase goods offered in the market. In this manner, both the government and small proprietors/businesses and consumers will benefit from the market operation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality, through the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board, expedite the revision of its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan and resubmit the same to the EMB for approval by the NSWM Commission. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MENRO to: » provide technical assistance to barangays without MRF to facilitate the establishment of the facility; » in coordination with the MPDC, ensure that the planned programs/projects for SWM have specific plans and are fully implemented during the year for the achievement of its objectives; » look for another foundation or organization that will accommodate the transfer of recyclables or adopt other disposal alternatives; and » request additional personnel/environmental police for a better monitoring of compliance of the public with the environment laws and ordinances and enforcement of penalties.
observations.managementAction: Planned PPAs related to SWM were implemented in CY 2017. The Municipality already coordinated with CEMEX Philippine Holdings to handle the Municipality’s recyclables. The SWM Officer commented that they will make representations with the EMB for the prioritization of the revision of their 10-year SWM Plan. He added that since the said plan was approved by the Sangguniang Bayan, they are currently using it as a guide in their implementation of the Municipality's SWM. Also, he informed that despite the Municipality's effort to allocate fund for the construction of MRF to its barangays, some barangays cannot locate or provide a government lot for its construction. Moreover, he reasoned out that the lack of EP is due to the Municipality’s personnel services limitation. Henceforth, he assured the Audit Team that they will tap the barangay officials to assign a deputized EP and give additional functions to other current municipal personnel to help in the implementation of their SWM-related ordinances.
observations.reason: The SWM Plan is still subject for revision to effect changes proposed by the EMB. The MENRO did not give any reason for its non-implementation. Additional EP was not provided in CY 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management instruct:
the MAO to conduct a thorough evaluation of the compliance of the beneficiaries of the Goat Dispersal Program with their undertakings under the same program; verify the cause of the death of the goats; require the submission of the pertinent documents to substantiate the same; and undertake other procedures that the circumstances may warrant;
the Municipal Accountant to record the death, after full compliance with the requirements for the dropping of accounts, and recognize the offsprings of the goats, after determining the fair market value less cost to sell, in the books of accounts; and
the Supply Officer and the MAO to fully account for the 28 heads of missing goats through appropriate reports, records and other documents to substantiate the same, and submit a written explanation for the cause of the discrepancies between the MR and records of receipts.

We recommended that the management: (a) limit the granting of cash advances to activities/expenditures specifically authorized under COA Circular No. 97 – 002 dated February 10, 1997. Discontinue the practice of purchasing goods out of cash advance; otherwise, the same shall be disallowed in audit; (b) authorize the granting of cash advance to elected municipal officials to the extent of their traveling expenses and similar claims only; and (c) issue demand letters to the concerned Municipal officials and employees for their unliquidated cash advances as at December 31, 2016; otherwise, institute appropriate administrative and legal actions against accountable officers who continually disregard the notices/ letters sent to them.

We recommended that the management:
instruct the BAC to: (i) ensure that its Resolution recommending the resort to alternative mode of procurement is justified and the grounds relied upon are those stated in the RIRR of R.A. No. 9184, and (ii) exert more effort in evaluating bid proposals and the qualifications of prospective bidders; be more cautious in declaring the lowest calculated and responsive bidder; and exercise more prudence in recommending award of contracts;
direct the BAC to submit to the Audit Team: (i) Minutes of Opening of Bids reportedly held on September 21, 2016 and October 4, 2016, respectively, and attendance sheet of the BAC members and observers, Abstract of Quotation, and BAC Resolution recommending and justifying to the Head of the Procuring Entity (HOPE) the use of alternative mode of procurement and approval by the HOPE of the BAC Resolution relative to the purchase of the utility van; and (ii) warranty securities of the two motor vehicles; otherwise, we shall be constrained to issue the corresponding Notice of Suspension (NS); and
refrain from authorizing payment of claims of suppliers/ contractors except when all pertinent supporting documents are attached thereto.

We recommended that the management (a) make a formal representation with the concerned suppliers and require them to submit the deficient documents that will enable the Municipality to apply for the insurance of the eleven (11) vehicles, and (b) apply for an insurance policy of the vehicles immediately after compliance by the said suppliers.

We recommended that the management:(a) require every League of Local Government Units, and League and Federation of Local Elective Officials to submit complete supporting documents prior to any release of financial aid; and (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the documents are complete before processing any claim thereof.

We recommended that the management stop any payment to private organization where municipal employees are members or affiliates thereto using the Municipal funds; otherwise, the Audit Team shall be constrained to disallow the same.

We recommended that the management (a) stop the practice of utilizing the 20% Development Fund for expenses deemed irrelevant to the intents of Item No. 4.0 of DILG – DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011 – 001 dated April 13, 2011; and (b) instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to determine available funds from the remaining 80% of IRA, specifically Capital Outlay, to recompense the 20% Development Fund for the amount of ₱2,089,299.47 incorrectly applied thereto.

We recommended that the management (a) carefully plan the projects, programs and activities that may be included in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan to see to it that only those items that are sanctioned and authorized by R.A. No. 10121 and NDRRMC–DBM–DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013 – 1 dated March 25, 2013 are included therein to prevent illegal and irregular disbursement of public funds; and (b) maintain a separate account with an authorized government depository bank for the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the management: (a) constantly follow – up its application for an ECC from the DENR to enable the MSWMB to fully and officially discharge its duties; (b) provide and require separate container for each type of waste from all sources and make sure that the same are properly marked or identified as "compostable", "non-recyclable", "recyclable" or "special waste", or any other classification to facilitate the sorting process and subsequent processing in the MRF; (c) revisit its Annual Budget for CY 2017 to determine the possibility of purchasing (i) a set of protective equipment for use of its collecting crews to avoid direct contact with hazardous wastes and (ii) one unit garbage truck with complete specifications and compliant with the requirements of the Ecological Management Act of 2000; and (d) request the Sangguniang Bayan to legislate appropriate Ordinances in aid of the Act and in the enforcement of its provisions, particularly on the prohibitions against open burning of solid wastes.

We recommended that the management revisit the approved budget of all its PPAs and ensure that a portion is set aside for the cost of signboards to be compliant with COA Circular No. 2013 – 004 dated January 30, 2013; and see to it that these are posted in conspicuous places within the agency premises, and in the venues where the PPAs are located or carried out to comply with the full disclosure policy.

We recommended that the:
management assign a particular municipal employee to monitor the continuing compliance and implementation of the provisions of the municipal tricycle code so that proper penalties may be strictly, promptly and immediately imposed and the appropriate fees collected against erring tricycle drivers/ operators for violations of the Candelaria Tricycle Code of 2010; and
Sanggunian Bayan revisit the Candelaria Tricycle Code of 2010 and incorporate the minimum standard requirements stated under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2007 – 01 dated January 2, 2007 for the safety of the drivers and passengers alike.

We recommended that the management: (a) make representation from ZAMECO I for the delivery of the transformer and facilitate the immediate installation thereof; (b)  commence the operation of the New Public Market as soon as the needed facilities are acquired; (c) post invitation on conspicuous places informing interested individuals of the commencement of operation of the New Public Market and encouraging them to lease stalls thereof; and (d) take actions to attract consumers to visit and buy goods offered in the market.
